fragility_points: Short-horizon BTC volatility could erase the cushion quickly.
Single-minute settlement makes the contract more fragile than a broader average-price rule.
Evidence is mostly about current regime persistence rather than a direct read on April 20 noon ET.
key_assumptions: BTC stays in its current trading regime and does not suffer an 8%+ drawdown into the April 20 noon ET settlement minute.
Binance remains operationally normal around the settlement window.
No hidden contract-interpretation issue overrides the stated one-minute close rule.
main_logical_chain: The governing venue is Binance BTC/USDT and current Binance spot is materially above 68k.
A base-rate prior for a threshold already 8% in-the-money over a six-day horizon favors YES.
Crypto short-horizon volatility and one-minute settlement mechanics still leave more tail risk than a 93.5% market price suggests.
Therefore YES remains likelier, but with a modest discount versus market confidence.
main_thesis: YES is likelier because BTC is already well above 68k on Binance, but a six-day crypto hold-above threshold is not quite as safe as the 93.5% market price implies.
quote_anchors: Polymarket rules: resolves on the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute candle close.
source_quality_view: Good enough for a medium-difficulty case: primary rules plus primary Binance data, cross-checked with CoinGecko. Independence is medium and source-of-truth ambiguity is low-to-medium.
strongest_disconfirmers: BTC can still fall more than 8% over six days, especially across a weekend.
The contract settles on one exact noon ET one-minute candle, which increases path and microstructure risk.
A Binance-specific anomalous print or operational issue could matter because Binance is the governing source.
strongest_supports: Binance ticker showed BTCUSDT around 74.23k on 2026-04-14, leaving about a 6.23k cushion above strike.
Recent Binance daily candles show BTC spending most of the recent month above 68k, with the latest cluster mostly above 70k.
CoinGecko independently showed BTC in the low-to-mid 70k area, reducing concern that Binance was a venue-specific outlier.
timing_relevance: High: the contract resolves at the Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-20, so both timezone mechanics and short-horizon volatility matter.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether weekend volatility or any macro shock emerges before settlement.
How much practical noise remains in Binance's timezone-sensitive noon ET candle handling.
what_would_change_view: I would cut materially if BTC loses the low-70k region before the weekend or if Binance-specific settlement risk rises; I would move closer to market if BTC still holds above roughly 72k into April 19-20 with no operational concerns.
